approach: in-session conductor holds the user channel; hitl at both gates
NOT additive: rewrites 2 frozen judge.feature scenarios (:11, :60) that assert the scalar four-field output; Clearance granted live by the owner, scoped to those two only
shared measurement instrument for every @rubric scenario in every ACED suite -> blast medium; callers keep a one-invoke contract so no suite fixes it locally
reversible: feature branch, PR-flow; freeze re-openable via async flag
self-reference at the impl gate: aced-impl-judge invokes aced-case-judge, the very subject under change -> name it, do not let it grade itself silently

why: Clearance granted live in-session by unional, scoped to TWO frozen judge.feature scenarios only ('invoked for one case it emits the four-field result', 'the output is exactly the four fields'): both assert the scalar four-field output the per-dimension contract contradicts, so no additive path exists. The other nine frozen scenarios stay untouched; structural gherkin-cli diff vs base confirms 2 removed / 0 modified / 9 unchanged. Owner also decided: blindness enforced inside case-judge (callers keep a one-invoke contract); simulator sees Given+When only; and the brief is composed by a deterministic engine rather than the judge's own judgment

cold spec-judge round 1 found the fix carried the same defect it was fixing: an implementation could spawn the blind simulator, DISCARD its return, and simulate-and-score in its own rubric-aware context while passing every blindness scenario. Closed by binding the scored artifact to the returned transcript. Round 2 found blindness bound brief-composition only, leaving retrieval-off-disk open; round 3 recused the engine node from ACED grading (deterministic, node:test-assertable, not LLM-graded) and proved its suite non-binding by ablation

Clearance ratified live by the owner, scoped to two frozen judge.feature scenarios asserting the scalar four-field output; structural gherkin-cli diff confirms 2 removed / 0 modified / 9 unchanged, so no untouched frozen scenario narrowed. Node judge: cold aced-spec-validator ALIGNED at round 3, all bars PASS -- a scalar impl now fails three independent scenarios, a one-context impl fails five; three prior rounds closed the transcript-provenance hole (an impl could spawn the blind context, discard its return, and score its own rubric-aware simulation while passing every blindness scenario), a dropped trigger-output shape, and a PASS-vs-must-not-do contradiction. Node extract-situation: NEW deterministic engine composing the simulator's brief; recused from ACED grading per aced-fit (assertable, not LLM-graded) so it carries no Fit line, following the sdd authoring/spec-gate engine-node precedent; its suite is boolean and node:test-bound, verified by mutation rather than by reading -- 13 of 24 mutations initially survived, all 13 now caught with a control mutation confirming the harness still reports survivors. Owner blessed the scope growth beyond the aced/case-judge touch-set. pnpm verify 21/21 green

cold impl-judge round 1 returned IMPLEMENTATION_PASS false with two blockers, both real. (1) the case-judge body bound only ONE conjunct of a frozen scenario -- 'a triggered must-not-do withholds the top score AND emits a non-passing verdict' -- its wording licensed emitting the top score with PASS no. (2) removing mean_score/std_dev from run's results JSON broke report, an unreconciled sibling consumer that also headlines a cross-suite mean the CR's own new body forbids. The judge additionally found the engine's CLI entrypoint guard uses import.meta.main (Node >=24.2) against an engines floor of >=22, where it is undefined: the script would print nothing and exit 0, and case-judge keyed its BLOCKER on a non-zero exit, so a supported Node would silently hand back an empty brief -- the exact eval-cannot-fail class this CR closes. Guard moved to the portable form already used by align-spec/check-scenario-overlap, covered by subprocess tests, ablation-proven. case-judge now treats an empty brief as a BLOCKER regardless of exit code

an independent non-ACED reader (brought in because the impl-judge invokes the subject and flagged its own independence as degraded) could not refute the blindness claim, but found the fix had severed the Then from the SCORING context as well as the simulating one. Must-not-do guards live only in Then steps; Pass 2 was told to read the rubric and never the Then, so the one hard fail in the frozen contract had no input and would have failed GREEN, invisibly -- the same eval-cannot-fail class this CR exists to close, reintroduced by its own fix. The asymmetry is now explicit: the simulating context is blind, the scoring context reads the whole scenario. Same reader found the engine skipped docstrings wholesale regardless of owning keyword, so a Given's docstring -- routinely the prompt under test -- silently vanished at exit 0 with a non-empty brief, catching neither guard; docstrings now inherit their owning step's fate. Also closed: no boolean-scenario branch (a regression from the prior body, and a contract gap), a trigger outline's N rows collapsing into one INVOKE (engine gained --row; one row is one case), an unnamed dispatch-failure path, an unresolved skill-dir placeholder, and a bare `node` invocation against a Node 22 engines floor. Both frozen suites took only ADDITIVE scenarios (addOnly true, 0 modified) so no second Clearance was required

impl-judge round 2 proved the CLI guard fix REINTRODUCED the identical defect: import.meta.url percent-encodes but the naive file:// template literal does not, so any script path holding a space made the guard never fire -- prints nothing, exits 0, silent empty brief. Same class, trigger moved from a Node version to an install path; verified live. Now pathToFileURL, covered by a spaced-path subprocess test and a source-level assertion (the import.meta.main revert is UNTESTABLE at runtime -- a test only ever probes the Node it runs on, and the defect lives on the >=22 floor). Round 2 also ablation-proved four extract-situation scenarios only PARTIALLY bound (placeholder tokens intact; empty-situation emits-no-brief; missing-arg usage-error; writes-no-other-file) -- each passed with the behavior removed. Fixed, and both remaining survivors now caught by source-level assertions mirroring verify-scenarios.test.mts

impl-judge round 2 found the CR broke report.feature:78, a FROZEN scenario in eval-run/report -- a node outside the declared touch-set and outside the ratified Clearance -- by deleting the Mean column from report/SKILL.md, leaving 'it shows each suite's pass rate, mean, and trend' unsatisfiable. Reconciled legally without a re-open: the frozen 'mean' is unqualified, so report now renders a NORMALIZED mean (%max: each rubric scenario's total over its own maximum, averaged), which satisfies the scenario and is the operation the CR's own thesis permits -- only the mean of RAW totals across differing maxima is the category error. Also swept the corpus surfaces the fix had not reached: glossary/README.md still stated PASS as the identity score>=threshold with no must-not-do exception AND claimed a rubric never appears in a .feature (stale since the inline-rubric CR); report/README.md, compare's regression example, define-governance, and run/report's 'explanation' field (which case-judge emits nothing for -- it emits WHAT WORKED/WHAT FAILED and forbids more)

impl-judge round 3 (cold, committed tree, judge.feature 30/30 and the prior eight findings closed) found the FIFTH recurrence of this CR's own defect class: the entire Examples-rendering path was bound by no test that could fail. Every assertion on rendered Examples output was NEGATIVE; the positive ones asserted the Situation DATA STRUCTURE, not the brief the simulator actually receives. formatTable could return empty and stay 62/62 green -- collapsing every trigger row to the literal placeholder so all rows simulate identically, which is precisely the failure --row was added to fix. Bound now by positive assertions on formatMarkdown's output; the judge's five surviving mutations (D1-D5) all fail, control survives. The judge's systemic note is the durable finding: 3 of 3 rounds found data-structure assertions standing in for output assertions on this engine -- a Then that says 'emitted' can only be bound by the rendered output, and asserting on an exported internal never binds it

a final 97-mutation sweep refuted the binding claim a fifth time: 28 survived, 16 real. NONE were engine defects -- the engine was correct in every case; the tests failed to bind it. Two mattered most. (1) every fixture opened with a bare `Feature: f` while the ENTIRE real corpus opens with @frozen, so no test ever fed the engine a real-corpus-shaped file -- constructed fixtures blind to the corpus they police. (2) the Examples keep-filter is exact-match and correct, but no fixture carried prefix-colliding columns (query vs query_expected), so a naive prefix 'fix' would leak the answer column silently. The internal-vs-output pattern MIGRATED a fourth time into formatJson, where every assertion was a negative sentinel check -- formatJson could return '[]' at full green. 14 of 16 now caught by ablation with a control still surviving; the remaining 2 (unanchored pipe match, slice(1,-1)) are equivalent mutants, unreachable in well-formed Gherkin since no step line follows an Examples block. Tests 66 -> 79

impl-judge round 4: judge.feature 30/30, extract-situation 27/27 on BEHAVIOR, prior blocker closed (the five Examples-rendering mutations now all die, control survives). It then found the SIXTH recurrence in a new seam: main() catches every error, so assert.throws on the pure function is a PROXY -- it cannot see an exit code or stdout. Swallowing RowOutOfRange/Ambiguous/Unparseable to return 0 WITH a brief survived at 79/79 green, while the same catch block's ScenarioNotFound/EmptySituation died -- proving the gap was purely coverage, not engine. Row-out-of-range defeats BOTH of case-judge's guards (non-zero exit, empty brief), so a wrong Examples row would be scored silently green. It also REFUTED my equivalence claim for includes('|') with a case I had missed: Gherkin COMMENTS are legal anywhere and a commented-out Examples row is a standard idiom -- my rationale ('no step line follows an Examples block') never considered that a comment is not a step line. Under includes('|') the commented row resurrects as live data, shifting every --row index. Engine handles it correctly; nothing bound it. All six now ablation-proven caught. Tests 79 -> 88. THE DURABLE RULE: a Then naming a PROCESS fact (exits non-zero, emits no brief) is bound only by driving the process; the same shape as round 3's 'asserting on an exported internal never binds an emission Then'

cold impl-judge round 4 on a committed, frozen tree: judge.feature 30/30, extract-situation 27/27 on behavior, scope clean, addOnly verified structurally. Its sole blocker -- three fail-closed Thens bound only by a proxy (assert.throws on a pure function under a catching main(), which cannot observe an exit code or stdout) -- is closed and proven by the judge's OWN ablations: swallowing RowOutOfRange/Ambiguous/Unparseable now fails, while the in-family controls still discriminate. It also refuted my equivalence claim for includes('|'): Gherkin comments are legal anywhere and a commented-out Examples row is a standard idiom, so my rationale ('no step line follows an Examples block') was wrong -- a comment is not a step line. Now bound. Convergence measured before stopping: rounds 1-2 plus an independent reader found 6 engine/body defects; rounds 3, 4 and a 97-mutation sweep found ZERO, only harness gaps, and the seam migrated each round (rendering -> formatJson -> the CLI error path), so another round finds another seam rather than converging. 88 tests, pnpm verify 21/21. RATIFIED by unional at auto-none leash. Independence is DEGRADED and recorded as such: the impl-judge's prescribed method is to invoke aced-case-judge, which IS the subject, so it refused as circular and read by hand -- every judge.feature verdict is a static contract-binding read, not a measurement, and the protocol has never been executed (filed blocking)

contradicts: this CR claims the eval-cannot-fail defect in aced-case-judge is closed -- but that claim rests entirely on a static contract-binding read. The protocol has NEVER been executed.
note: Name how ACED grades its own runner. aced-impl-judge's prescribed method is to invoke aced-case-judge; when case-judge IS the subject, that is circular and a broken protocol would certify itself. It correctly refused and read by hand, so 30/30 is a read, not a measurement. This is a one-off recursion (ACED's runner is itself an ACED subject), NOT a general bootstrap mechanism to add to the system. Narrow options: drive the new protocol from a plain non-ACED subagent with a sentinel-laced scenario and assert the simulator's transcript never contains the sentinel; or grade the new body with the previous version from main.

The spec gate has no ablation step. check-suite proves FORM and the cold judge READS; neither ever asks 'does a Given exist under which this Then fails?'. All six eval-cannot-fail recurrences in this CR hid in exactly that gap, and every one was found by mutation, never by reading -- reading yielded 1-2 findings per pass while mutation sweeps yielded 13, 16, and 28.

A binding bar, split by subject type. DETERMINISTIC engines: a standard mutation-operator library gives a real mutation score mechanically in CI -- do not bound it. PROSE subjects: bound the operator set, because unbounded semantic mutation needs a corpus or a trained model and is out of scope. Start from the six classes this CR observed: apparatus (the Given chosen so the Then cannot discriminate), internal-proxy (asserting an exported internal instead of the rendered output), half-conjunct (one clause of a multi-clause Then bound), process-fact (assert.throws under a catching main cannot see an exit code), runtime-reach (the defect lives where the test runtime cannot go), corpus-blind (fixtures shaped unlike the real corpus). CRITICAL: a bounded check that reports PASS is the same false-green class it exists to catch -- the verdict must NAME its bound ('killed 6/6 of {list}'), never 'the suite binds'. The list RATCHETS: every future escape adds an operator. Caveat: six observations from one CR against one artifact -- a starting list, not a closed ontology. Mutants must not come from the author: I ablated my own fixes and they held, and I also wrote an equivalence claim that a 97-mutation sweep agreed with and a cold judge refuted in one shot. Two derived laws: a Then naming a PROCESS fact (exits non-zero, emits no brief) is bound only by driving the process; a Then naming EMISSION is bound only by the rendered output.

~23 repo scripts guard their CLI entrypoint with `import.meta.main`, which is Node >=24.2, against an engines.node floor of '>=22' where it is undefined -- the script prints nothing and exits 0, and a caller keying its BLOCKER on a non-zero exit reads that as success. Fix MECHANICALLY in check:ci (a grep or lint rule), never as a prose bar telling a judge to hand-read 23 scripts: judging programming-language syntax by reading it as prose is wasteful and unreliable. Either raise the floor to >=24.2 or move to the pathToFileURL form already used by align-spec/place-node/check-scenario-overlap. Note the naive `file://${process.argv[1]}` concat is NOT a fix -- import.meta.url percent-encodes, so it breaks on any path holding a space.

No node fences the results-JSON contract between run (producer) and report/compare (consumers) -- the artifact this CR reshaped hardest, and where its only cross-node break landed (report consumed mean_score after run stopped writing it). All three are prose skills with no executable surface, so nothing detects the drift. Concrete open instance: run/SKILL.md gives a trigger row its own 'pass' inside the scenarios array while trigger pass is defined as accuracy aggregated ACROSS rows -- the pass/fail unit (row vs outline) is ambiguous.

check-suite's RUBRIC_EXEMPT_RE is a lexical guard with an accidental escape hatch: a step clears the rubric-noun ban by containing 'pass'/'verdict'/'no' anywhere in it. Steps that are strictly MORE boolean can fail while less boolean ones pass, so it would exempt a genuine leak by the same accident that exempted honest steps. A check that cannot classify must escalate, not exempt.

extract-situation withholds a docstring by its owning step's keyword, so blindness depends entirely on suite-format-governance Form 2 mandating the rubric live in a Then docstring. A non-conforming suite placing a rubric under a Given/When would have the engine EMIT the rubric to the blind simulator -- the exact leak this CR closes. The engine cannot detect it; enforcement lives at the spec gate.

Docs and fixtures still ship the retired 1-5 scalar contract: apps/website/src/content/docs/aced/{run,report,overview,add-scenario}.md (live copy advertising 'mean score +/- standard deviation'), docs/specs/aced/design.md, artifacts/specs/aced-plugin/spec.md, and ~140 golden-set/*.md fixtures. Documentation domain -- likely a Quill node.
